ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for the composite braised abalone and shiitake claypot dish. FOODON_03315595 (abalone food product) is used as the closest single-ingredient anchor. The shiitake and claypot preparation aspects are not captured by this term.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00GJ (Abalone) is used as the primary ingredient anchor. No composite FoodEx2 code exists for braised abalone with shiitake mushrooms in claypot preparation. Shiitake component is not captured.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite braised seafood dish. SNOMED CT does not provide food composition codes at this level of specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Abalone and Shiitake Claypot is approximately 200 g, comprising braised abalone pieces, thick-capped shiitake mushrooms, and the gelatinous braising sauce.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Abalone, mixed species, raw (FDC ID 174183) and Mushrooms, shiitake, cooked (FDC ID 169242); values combined and adapted for braised composite dish with oyster sauce and chicken stock
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for the composite Abalone and Shiitake Claypot dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for raw abalone (FDC ID 174183) and cooked shiitake mushrooms (FDC ID 169242), blended in approximate proportions (abalone ~40%, shiitake ~35%, sauce ~25%) and adjusted upward for sodium to reflect oyster sauce and chicken stock contribution. Energy, protein, fat, and carbohydrate values are consistent with the input record and cross-checked against USDA component data. Saturated fat estimated from USDA abalone and mushroom fat profiles. The input record's per_100g values (energy 115 kcal, protein 9.2 g, fat 3.5 g, carbohydrate 8.8 g) are plausible for this composition and are retained with medium confidence.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Mixed Seafood and Mushroom Dishes — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for braised shellfish and high-fibre mushroom dishes
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Abalone and Shiitake Claypot in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. GI of 35 (low) is estimated by analogy with braised shellfish and high-protein, high-fibre mushroom dishes, which consistently fall in the low GI range (30–45) due to high protein content slowing gastric emptying and dietary fibre from shiitake mushrooms moderating glucose absorption. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (35 × 17.6) / 100 = 6.2 (low). Serving size 200 g; carbohydrate per serving = 8.8 × 2 = 17.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (480 mg per 100 g; ~960 mg per standard 200 g serving) from oyster sauce and chicken stock in the braising liquid
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 100 g or fewer, request a reduced-sodium preparation omitting or minimising oyster sauce, and account for this dish's sodium contribution within a daily target of under 1500–2000 mg sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35) and moderate carbohydrate content (8.8 g per 100 g), with high-quality protein from abalone and dietary fibre from shiitake mushrooms supporting glycaemic control
type2_diabetes.guidance: Abalone and Shiitake Claypot is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes at a standard 200 g serving, but the cornstarch thickener in the sauce should be monitored; requesting a lighter sauce or smaller portion reduces the glycaemic load to well within acceptable limits.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (480 mg per 100 g) raises cardiovascular concern, partially offset by low saturated fat (~0.7 g per 100 g), beneficial selenium (35 mcg per 100 g), and the anti-inflammatory polysaccharides in shiitake mushrooms
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease may consume this dish occasionally in a 100–150 g portion, prioritising lower-sodium preparation methods and balancing daily sodium intake accordingly, while benefiting from the lean protein and micronutrient profile of abalone and shiitake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Shiitake mushrooms contain lentinan and beta-glucan polysaccharides with documented anti-inflammatory properties; abalone provides selenium supporting antioxidant defence
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This dish is generally well-tolerated for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; sodium intake should be balanced within daily limits to avoid fluid retention that may exacerbate joint swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Abalone and Shiitake Claypot (鲍鱼花菇煲, Bàoyú huāgū bāo) is a prestigious Cantonese and Fujian banquet dish celebrated for its luxurious umami depth and the remarkable textural harmony between premium thick-capped shiitake mushrooms (huagu, 花菇) and tender braised abalone. The dish is prepared by slow-braising abalone and rehydrated huagu mushrooms in a rich, gelatinous sauce made from oyster sauce, chicken stock, and ham essence in a traditional claypot, which ensures even heat distribution and deep flavour penetration. The hallmark of a masterfully prepared version is the near-indistinguishable texture between the mushroom caps and the abalone — both yielding a tender yet slightly chewy bite enveloped in a glossy, savoury glaze. Nutritionally, the dish provides approximately 115 kcal per 100 g, with 9.2 g protein, 3.5 g fat, and 8.8 g carbohydrate. It is a meaningful source of vitamin B12 (1.2 mcg), selenium (35 mcg), niacin (3.5 mg), and zinc (1.5 mg) per 100 g. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 35 (low), making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions. However, the sodium content of 480 mg per 100 g — driven by oyster sauce and chicken stock — makes it a high-concern dish for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease unless prepared with reduced-sodium ingredients. Clinically, the dish offers lean protein, low saturated fat, and anti-inflammatory compounds from shiitake polysaccharides, positioning it as a nutritionally valuable but sodium-intensive celebratory food.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for the composite Abalone and Shiitake Claypot dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for raw abalone and cooked shiitake mushrooms, adjusted for sauce contribution. The input record's values are plausible and consistent with USDA component data but cannot be verified against a direct approved-source match. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central (abalone FDC 174183; shiitake cooked FDC 169242) in approximate dish proportions; no direct approved-source composite entry exists for this dish. 2. Saturated fat estimated at 0.7 g per 100 g based on USDA fat profiles for abalone and shiitake; not reported in input record. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation, cholesterol, and moisture data for this braised composite dish are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2026-04-16 for this specific preparation. 4. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database principles applied to high-protein, high-fibre braised shellfish dishes; no direct GI measurement exists for this dish. 5. Glycemic load per serving = (35 × 17.6) / 100 = 6.2 (low); serving size 200 g, carbohydrate per serving 17.6 g. 6. Duplicate portion entry in input record corrected to single default serving of 200 g. 7. The input source (Fujian Agriculture and Forestry University) is not on the approved source list; values have been cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central and retained where plausible. 8. Halal status marked false as abalone preparation with oyster sauce and ham essence requires verification of halal-certified ingredients; default conservative assessment applied.
